prose:
Method provenance
Property: HOMO–LUMO (frontier-orbital) gap of ethylene, in electron-volts (eV)
Level of theory: DFT, functional B3LYP with the def2-TZVP Gaussian basis set (no density-fitting: exact four-center integrals)
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (ethylene.xyz, 6 atoms); single-point calculation (no geometry relaxation) → notation B3LYP/def2-TZVP//input xyz
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent specified)
Charge = 0 (neutral); multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet, treated with restricted Kohn–Sham)
Software: PySCF (called via chemkit frontier-orbitals skill); frontier-orbital analysis performed by chemkit vX (backend pyscf)
SCF convergence: achieved in 6 cycles; integrity.status = ok, trustworthy = true; no warnings reported by engine
Live log: written during execution to a file named frontier_<timestamp>.out in the working directory (announced at job start); result JSON: /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpiio_4dvo/live.json

Result
HOMO energy  =  –7.6155818 eV
LUMO energy  =  –0.17986527 eV
→ HOMO–LUMO gap = 7.4357165 eV  (Koopmans-style orbital gap, not an excitation energy)
